key_files.intent: Implements run_cycle(), the primary SDK entry point that drives one full orchestration cycle: requirement patching, workset selection, concept collection, planning, graph validation, dispatch, and verification.
Defines RuntimeState dataclass: the mutable operational state passed between all graph nodes in a single cycle.
Pure node functions (project_scheduler_node, concept_collection_node, planning_and_shaping_node, graph_validation_node) that mutate RuntimeState and are composable into LangGraph graphs.
Builds the formal LangGraph StateGraph (build_meta_graph) wiring all nodes with conditional edges and requirement-patch branching.
Declares META_GRAPH_NODES tuple and NEXT_STEP_BY_EVENT dict as the canonical node-name registry for the meta-graph.
next_step_for_state() resolves the next coarse meta-step from RuntimeState flags (termination, user input, patches, workset presence).
Extended node variants that inject tool-enriched context (Brave Search, Stitch UI) into planning and design decisions.
Primary runtime integration tests: fixture cycles, dispatch assertions, execution ref shape, event sequence, and retry/fallback routing.
Cross-scenario convergence tests: acceptance pass terminates, acceptance fail triggers replan, design backloop, seam compliance, state consistency.
End-to-end test using game_project fixture: verifies full cycle including knowledge selection, dispatch, and snapshot mutation.

Defines the ExecutorAdapter Protocol (supports_phase, supports_role, estimate, prepare_request, submit, dispatch, poll, cancel, normalize_result) plus ExecutorDispatch and SubmissionReceipt dataclasses.
Exports all adapters, registers them into EXECUTOR_REGISTRY at import time, and re-exports the full public surface.

Concrete adapter implementations: BaseExecutorAdapter, CodexAdapter (codex exec --full-auto), ClaudeCodeAdapter (claude --print --output-format json), PythonAdapter (local_runner), ClineAdapter, OpenCodeAdapter, TopologyClassifierAdapter.
SubprocessTransport wraps subprocess.Popen with submit/poll/cancel lifecycle; build_claude_code_command, build_codex_command, build_python_local_command construct the CLI argv arrays.
EXECUTOR_CAPABILITIES registry maps executor names to ExecutorCapability (context_window, max_package_tokens, granularity, supported_phases, max_concurrent).
format_executor_payload() builds the structured payload dict injected into each executor request, including knowledge packet, pull manifest, and style metadata.
Defines PullPolicyRule and PULL_POLICY_RULES; resolve_pull_strategy() selects context pull mode/budget per executor+role combination.
EXECUTOR_REGISTRY dict and register_executor_adapter/get_executor_adapter functions for dynamic adapter lookup.
Granularity control: validate_granularity, estimate_package_tokens, suggest_split, suggest_merge for capacity-aware work package sizing.
Pure-Python deterministic executor invoked via subprocess as a fallback when live AI executors are unavailable; handles acceptance report generation.
Stub adapter implementations used in test mode to simulate executor responses without calling real CLI tools.
Tests format_executor_payload, pull strategy resolution, and runtime context injection shape for all executor types.
Tests SubprocessTransport submit/poll/cancel lifecycle and command builder functions.
Tests EXECUTOR_CAPABILITIES entries and get_executor_capability fallback behavior.
Tests local_runner acceptance report generation and deterministic execution path.
Re-exports all domain model types and codec functions as the single import surface for the state layer.
WorkPackage dataclass: bounded execution unit with status, executor, retry state, findings, deliverables, and dependency tracking.
ProjectState dataclass: project phases, domains, roles, seams, work package IDs, and per-project LLM/knowledge/pull preferences.
InitiativeState dataclass: top-level initiative owning multiple projects with shared concepts, contracts, and requirement event IDs.
WorkspaceState and QueueState dataclasses: top-level workspace with global scheduler queues and registry references.
decode_snapshot/encode_snapshot: converts raw JSON dicts to/from typed domain model dataclasses, the single serialization boundary.
SeamState and SeamRisk dataclasses: cross-domain interface contract tracking with risk level and verification status.
ExecutorPolicy dataclass with resolve() method: per-project routing rules that override work package executor assignments by domain/phase/role.
ProductDesign, DomainSpec, UserFlow, ClosureItem dataclasses for structured product design artifacts.
AcceptanceVerdict, GoalCheckResult, GapItem, RemediationPackage, ClosureDensityScore dataclasses for acceptance evaluation results.
NodeRevision, NodeProgress, NodeLineage, NodeScope: evolving state node tracking for fine-grained work sub-items.
Sample game project snapshot fixture: canonical example of a complete WorkspaceState document used in e2e and integration tests.
Sample e-commerce project snapshot fixture: second canonical WorkspaceState example covering a different project archetype.
Tests WorkPackage, ProjectState, InitiativeState field defaults, status transitions, and role registry lookups.
Tests decode_snapshot/encode_snapshot round-trip fidelity for all entity types.
Re-exports all planning decision functions as the single import surface for the planning layer.
llm_concept_collection_decider(): LLM-backed structured decision for which knowledge areas and questions to focus on in the current phase.
llm_planning_decider(): LLM-backed workset selection decision that confirms or reorders the scheduler-proposed work packages.
decide_retry_action() and llm_retry_decider(): LLM-backed retry routing (switch_executor, requeue, block, replan, final_fail) after executor failure.
resolve_retry_action(): rule-based retry policy (attempt count, executor availability) as guardrail before the LLM retry decider.
apply_requirement_events(), apply_patch_operations(), apply_project_split(), freeze_seam(), verify_seam(): mutation operations applied to the snapshot JSON structure.
generate_product_design(): LLM-backed ProductDesign generation from initiative/project context.
validate_design(): structural and semantic validation of ProductDesign (circular domain deps, missing flows, domain coverage).
expand_closures(): enumerates ring-0 and ring-1 task closures from a ProductDesign for coverage ratio computation.
analyze_gaps(): LLM-backed gap attribution and remediation package generation when acceptance goals are not met.
evaluate_acceptance(): LLM-backed acceptance verdict combining goal completion, closure density, and work package results.
Tests llm_planning_decider with mock LLM: workset selection, phase assignment, and fallback behavior.
Tests decide_retry_action routing across all retry actions including switch_executor and final_fail.
Tests product_design_node, design_validation_node, closure_expansion_node as graph node functions.
Tests evaluate_acceptance verdict logic with passing and failing work package result sets.
Re-exports all persistence classes and factory functions as the single import surface.
Protocol definitions for SnapshotStore, EventStore, ArtifactStore, and MemoryStore: the abstract persistence contracts.
WorkspacePersistence dataclass and build_local_workspace_persistence(): assembles SQLite + filesystem persistence layout under .devforge/.
SQLiteSnapshotStore and SQLiteEventStore: durable SQLite-backed snapshot (latest + history) and event log.
sqlite_schema() returns DDL statements for snapshots and events tables; SQLITE_SCHEMA_STATEMENTS constant.
JsonlEventStore: lightweight append-only JSONL event log as alternative to SQLite for low-overhead deployments.
JsonStore: file-based snapshot store that reads/writes JSON files from a directory (used for fixture loading).
JsonMemoryStore: JSON file-based memory store for persisted planning context (concept briefs, specialized knowledge).
FileArtifactStore: filesystem-backed text artifact store writing to .devforge/artifacts/ for executor outputs and runtime context.
Tests all four store implementations against real filesystem/SQLite: round-trip snapshot, event append, artifact write, memory save.
LLMClient Protocol: provider-agnostic interface with generate_structured(StructuredGenerationRequest) -> StructuredGenerationResponse.
Re-exports all LLM client types, factory functions, and transport classes as the single LLM import surface.
OpenRouterClient: implements LLMClient over OpenRouter's chat-completions API using httpx transport.
GoogleGenAIClient: implements LLMClient over Google's Gemini generateContent API using httpx transport.
build_llm_client() and build_llm_client_from_config(): factory functions that instantiate the correct provider client from preferences dict.
build_task_llm_client(): routes LLM task requests to the appropriate provider based on llm_preferences + llm.yaml config.
load_llm_config(): discovers and parses llm.yaml from the project root for provider/model preferences.
HttpxTransport: production HTTP transport using httpx for all LLM provider API calls.
MockLLMClient: deterministic test double that returns structured stub responses without network calls.
Example LLM provider configuration file specifying provider, model, and allow_live flag for runtime LLM routing.
Tests build_task_llm_client routing logic across provider preferences and fallback behavior.
Re-exports all external tool client types as the single tools import surface.
BraveSearchClient: calls Brave Search API (BRAVE_API_KEY) for market research and competitive analysis during concept collection.
FalImageClient: calls fal.ai API (FAL_KEY) for Flux/SDXL image generation used in UI mockups and design artifacts.
StitchClient: calls Google Stitch API for AI-driven UI prototype generation in HTTP or MCP mode.
XVValidator: cross-model validation that submits the same prompt to multiple LLM providers and compares structured outputs.
Tests all tool client stubs and result model shapes (BraveSearchClient, FalImageClient, StitchClient, XVValidator).
select_workset(): priority-sorted, dependency-filtered selection of up to N ready WorkPackages for batch dispatch.
ContextBroker: resolves project://, artifact://, memory:// URI refs by dispatching to snapshot, artifact_store, or memory_store; supports preview_many() and resolve_many().
ContextPullManifest and ResolvedContext dataclasses: represent the ref list and resolved content injected into executor payloads.
Tests ContextBroker ref resolution for all URI schemes and preview modes against mock stores.
Re-exports KNOWLEDGE_REGISTRY, KnowledgeDocument, NodeKnowledgePacket, and selection/specializer functions.
KNOWLEDGE_REGISTRY: maps knowledge IDs (domain.gaming, phase.implementation) to KnowledgeDocument instances.
select_knowledge_for_context(): heuristic selection of relevant knowledge IDs given project archetype, phase, and role.
build_specialized_knowledge(): assembles a structured focused knowledge dict from selected document IDs for injection into executor payloads.
build_node_knowledge_packet(): creates the NodeKnowledgePacket struct with focus, deep_refs, and phase-specific guidance for a graph node.
Tests knowledge selection, specializer output shape, and packet assembly for multiple phase/role/domain combinations.
RoleSpec dataclass with name, description, and executor_phase_affinity for each development role.
ROLE_REGISTRY dict and get_role_spec() lookup for the six standard roles used across all projects.
verify_seam_compliance(): checks SeamState interface contracts against executor work package outputs for integration risk reporting.
Tests seam compliance verification logic for satisfied and violated interface contracts.
load_project_config(), apply_project_config(), maybe_apply_fixture_project_config(): reads devforge.project_config.json and overlays per-project preferences onto snapshots.
Example project config file for the e-commerce fixture showing llm_preferences, knowledge_preferences, and pull_policy_overrides structure.
Tests project config loading, overlay application, and fixture config discovery.
classify_workspace_candidates() uses an LLM to decide single_project vs workspace topology; WorkspaceCandidate and WorkspaceModelingDecision carry the decision.
CLI entrypoint (main) and public SDK functions: run_fixture_cycle, run_snapshot_cycle, initialize_project, run_executor_doctor; dispatches to subcommands init/snapshot/fixture/doctor.
Interactive REPL session: run_interactive_session() and persist_session_bundle() for conversational cycle continuation from the terminal.
SessionState and ViewState dataclasses for tracking active REPL session (project, phase, run IDs, mode).
read_readme_excerpt(): extracts a README snippet used during workspace topology discovery.
Package metadata, Python 3.12 constraint, LangGraph/httpx dependencies, devforge CLI entry point, and pytest configuration.
Tests all CLI subcommands (init, snapshot, fixture, doctor) including guided preferences, workspace mode, and JSON output.
